title: Sub-ARTIC Illumina SARS-CoV-2 Spike sequencing protocol (LoCost) V3.2
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8rluo","text":"This protocol describes a procedure for sequencing the Spike region of SARS-CoV-2 using short amplicons (146-208bp). The method has proved to successful with both clinical RNA samples and degraded wastewater samples. The primers are unique to this method. The library prep procedure has been heavily adapted from the ncov-2019 sequencing v3 (ARTIC) protocol by Josh Quick (https://www.protocols.io/view/ncov-2019-sequencing-protocol-v3-locost-bh42j8ye) and the \"low cost\" method from the NEOF Liverpool Illumina ARTIC protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":373,"length":78}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"5BFC52C1421A11ECA9C70A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/ncov-2019-sequencing-protocol-v3-locost-bh42j8ye"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cm8n6","text":"Before starting, generate the \"odd\" and \"even\" primer pools as follows:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ci7om","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gq6m","text":"1. Fully resuspend lyophilised oligonucleotides in 1x TE to a concentration of 100 micromolar (µM) , vortex thoroughly and spin down ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1l164","text":"2. Sort the odd and even primer sets into separate batches and label two 1.5-ml tubes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vk8","text":"3. Starting with the even primer set add the volume (μl) given overleaf to the pooled 1.5-ml tube (between 7.5 - 15). Repeat with the odds. Vortex and spin down. These are your pooled stocks. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3cr0c","text":"4. Dilute the pools one in ten across several aliquots with molecular grade water. Vortex and spin down. These are your working primer pools. steps: Primers are separated into two pools, odd and even, depending on where they sit across the Spike region. See the guidelines section for further details. In a clean pre-PCR hood set up two PCR reactions, one per pool as follows: A B C 1 Component Rxn 1 Rxn 2 2 Pool Even 0 µl 1.75 µl 3 Pool Odd 1.75 µl 0 µl 4 Q5 Hot Start Hi-Fi 2x Master Mix 6.25 µl 6.25 µl 5 Total 8 µl 8 µl

Prepare a master mix of the reagents as below by multiplying volumes by the number of samples; add 10% to allow for pipetting error. A B 1 Component Vol/PCR Rxn 2 NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 0.6 µl 3 NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 1.4 µl 4 Total 2 µl

Prepare adapter ligation master mix by adding volumes as detailed below for each sample; adding 10% to allow for pipetting error. A B 1 Component Vol/PCR rxn 2 NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix 6 µl 3 NEBNext Ligation Enhancer 0.2 µl 4 NEBNext Adapter 0.5 µl 5 Total 6.7 µl
Increase volume of sample from 19.7 µL to 25 µL by the addition of nuclease-free water.

Add the following components to separate wells of a PCR plate. Make sure to use the indexes in appropriate wells so that each sample has unique i5 and i7 indexes. If using less than 12 samples, the i5 primer can be replaced by the Universal PCR primer. A B 1 Component Vol/Rxn 2 Adapter ligated cDNA fragments 7.5 µl 3 NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 12.5 µl 4 Index Primer i7 2.5 µl 5 Index Primer i5* 2.5 µl 6 Total 25 µl * if using 12 samples or fewer replace index primer i5 with the Universal primer.

Pool 5 µL of each sample together in a fresh low-binding microfuge tube and mix. This will give a final volume of 60 µL for twelve samples. If larger numbers of samples are used then pool 5 µL of each and mix. Split into aliquots of about 120 µL .

Perform qPCR by using a Kapa Library Quant kit for Illumina from Roche. Reactions are scaled down to 10 µL . Use the appropriate Kapa kit for the type of qPCR machine used, see https://pim-eservices.roche.com/eLD/api/downloads/ca670ceb-fb38-eb11-0291-005056a71a5d?countryIsoCode=pi
Take 5 µL of 4 nanomolar (nM) dilution of library and add 5 µL of 0.1 Molarity (M) NaOH to denature the DNA and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Add 4.5 µL of cDNA to respective wells to give a total volume of 12.5 µL . Include negative controls. Mix by pipetting, seal plate and centrifuge briefly.

Perform PCR using the following program (with heated lid): A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 s 1 3 Denaturation 98°C 15 s 35 4 Annealing & Extension 60°C 5 min 5 Hold 4°C Hold Fewer PCR cycles can be used for samples of higher concentration.

In a freshly bleached post-PCR hood that has been subjected to UV for 00:40:00 , combine Pool Even and Pool Odd to give 20 µL in each tube/well. Add 20 µL nuclease-free water to dilute products 1 in 2. At least a 1 in 5 dilution is advised when using highly concentrated clincial samples subjected to a 35x cycle PCR.
Mix well by pipetting and centrifuge briefly.

Combine 2 µL of End Prep master mix with 10 µL of amplified cDNA and mix by pipetting. Spin down, seal, place in a thermocycler and run the following program: A B 1 Temperature Time 2 20°C 15 min 3 65°C 15 min 4 4°C Hold
Add 6.7 µL ligation mix to each 12 µL amplified cDNA/mastermix and mix by pipetting. Incubate at 20 °C for 00:15:00 .
Add 1 µL of USER enzyme to the ligation mixture. Mix by pipetting, centrifuge briefly and incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 .
Perform a 0.9x Ampure XP bead clean by adding 22.5 µL of Ampure XP beads and mix by pepetting. Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer tube/plate to magnet and allow beads to clump for 00:05:00 . Remove supernatant taking care not to disturb pellet, and discard.
Wash the beads with 100 µL 80% ethanol for 00:00:30 then remove ethanol by pipetting. Repeat one more time making sure to remove any residual ethanol.
Allow beads to air dry for around 00:05:00 ; ensure that all the ethanol has evaporated. Do not let beads dry to the point of cracking, as this could affect the amount of DNA recovered.

Add 17 µL of nuclease-free water to each well and mix by pipetting. Allow to stand for 00:05:00 then transfer to magnet and allow beads to clump for 00:05:00 . Remove 15 µL into a fresh low-bind tube.
Perform Qubit assay to determine concentration.
Mix thoroughly by pipetting and centrifuge briefly.

Place on thermocycler and perform PCR using the following program: A B C D 1 Cycle Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 s 1 3 Denaturation 98°C 10 s 3 - 15* 4 Annealing/Extension 65°C 75 s 5 Final Extension 65°C 5 mins 1 6 Hold 4°C Hold *Refer to NEBNext protocol for number of cycles required. (Examples: 100 ng = ~3 cycles, 50 ng = ~3–4 cycles, 10 ng = ~6–7 cycles)
Perform a 0.7x bead clean by adding 42 µL or 84 µL magnetic beads, depending on sample quantity. Leave for 00:05:00 to allow beads to bind DNA.
Place on a magnet and allow beads to clump for 00:05:00 . Remove supernatant and discard.

Wash pellet with 100 µL 80% ethanol for 00:00:30 and remove. Repeat one more time. Allow the ethanol to dry off for about 00:05:00 , making sure the pelleted beads do not dry out, which can reduce DNA recovery.
Add 30 µL of nuclease-free water and mix by pipetting. Leave for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow DNA to elute from the beads.
Place the tube/plate on the magnet and let the beads clump for 00:05:00 . Elute DNA into a fresh low-binding tube.
Perform TapeStation assay to determine if primer dimers are present. If present, perform another 0.7x bead clean and check again.
Enter the values obtained from qPCR into the KAPA Library Quantification Data Analysis Template.
Neutralise with 5 µL of 200 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl pH 7.0 for 00:01:00 .
Take 35 µL of 20 picomolar (pM) sample and add 465 µL of HT1. This will give 500 µL of 1.4 picomolar (pM) sequencing mix.

In a freshly bleached Pre-PCR hood (ideally in an isolated clean room), that has been subjected to UV irradiation for 00:40:00 , add LunaScript and RNA sample to a tube/well as follows (making sure to include negative controls): A B 1 Component Vol/Rxn 2 LunaScript Super Mix 2 µl 3 RNA sample 8 µl
Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down several times, seal plate, and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate the reaction as follows (with heated lid): A B 1 Temperature Time 2 25°C 2 min 3 55°C 10 min 4 95°C 1 min 5 4°C Hold
Add 985 µL cold HT1 (hybridisation buffer) to give 20 picomolar (pM) denatured library
Thaw a tube of 10 nanomolar (nM) PhiX stock .

Combine the following volumes in a microcentrifuge tube: 10 nanomolar (nM) PhiX (10 μl) and RSB (Resuspension Buffer) (15 μl). The total volume is 25 μl at 4 nanomolar (nM) . Vortex briefly and then pulse centrifuge. The 4 nanomolar (nM) PhiX can be stored frozen for 3 months.
Combine 5 µL of 4 nanomolar (nM) PhiX with 5 μl of 0.1 Molarity (M) NaOH. Vortex briefly and pulse centrifuge. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
Add 5 µL of 200 nanomolar (nM) Tris-HCl ( pH 7.0 ), vortex briefly, pulse centrifuge and incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00
Add 985 µL of HT1 (hybridisation buffer) to give a concentration of 20 picomolar (pM) . Vortex briefly and pulse centrifuge.
Combine your library with PhiX control to give a final dilution of PhiX of 5%

Check the quality and concentration of negatives and a selection of samples using a fluorometer and/or Agilent TapeStation. Samples can be normalised at this point if even barcode representation is required, but at the cost of time.
Dilute sample to 4 nanomolar (nM) .
PhiX control at 20 picomolar (pM) can be frozen at -20°C for up to two weeks. After this time cluster numbers tend to decrease.
creator: Gavin J Horsburgh
